Challenge: Read the Scout Promise aloud to one teammate (tap "Show extra info" — only the reader may look!). They must tell it to the other teammate, who must repeat it back to the reader. No one may look up the promise or write it down. It must come back accurately or the challenge is failed. Fastest wins.
Info:
The Scout Promise:

I promise that I will do my best
to uphold our Scout values, to do my duty to The King,
to help other people
and to keep the Cub Scout Law.
Challenge (2p): Read the Scout Promise aloud to your teammate (tap "Show extra info" — only the reader may look!). They must then repeat it back to you accurately. No one is allowed to look up the Scout Promise or write it down. It must come back accurately or the challenge is failed. Fastest wins.

Challenge: Two teammates stand facing away from each other in complete silence, counting 75 seconds in their heads — the clock started the moment you pressed Start. The next thing either of them may say is "Time" when they believe 75 seconds have passed. When the first person says it, hit the finish button — the elapsed time is your result. Land within 5 seconds of 1:15 to pass; the time closest to 1:15 wins a steal.

Teammates A and B must invent a secret handshake with at least 10 steps. B then teaches it to C, who must not have seen or heard it being created. A and C must then perform the handshake with no mistakes. Fastest wins.
Pass Mark: A and C perform it perfectly
Challenge (2p): One teammate invents a secret handshake with at least 10 steps on their own and writes down every step. They then teach it to their partner. Your partner then has ONE attempt to perform the handshake back correctly — get it wrong and the challenge is failed. Fastest wins.
Pass Mark (2p): Partner performs it correctly on their first attempt

Challenge: Find Joe. Fastest wins.
Make an A4-sized target. Standing five metres away, throw stones (each over 2cm across) at it — every stone that lands on the target scores one point. You have 5 minutes. Most points wins.
Guess the distance from the edge of the arena stage to the till of the scout shop, in metres — you can include the centimetres, e.g. "38.25". No measuring! Closest guess wins.
Find five different types of leaves from trees or bushes. Fastest wins.

Act out the 5 animals hidden under "Show extra info" — only the actor may look! No sounds or talking allowed except confirming a correct guess, and your team must say the EXACT name of each animal. Any talking or unauthorised communication is a fail. Fastest wins.
Info:
Act these out, in order:

1. Elephant
2. Butterfly
3. Vulture
4. Spider
5. Chimpanzee

Challenge: Standing at least 10 metres apart, one teammate signals random letters in semaphore; the other must call each letter correctly. Attempt as many letters as you like, but every attempted letter must be read correctly. Most letters wins.
Find five insects (they must be insects). Fastest wins.
Find a spiderweb. Fastest wins.
Find three sticks that are exactly the same length. You cannot break sticks off trees or snap them to change their length. Fastest wins.
What year did the Scout Association originally purchase the land for Bushy Wood Activity Centre? No research allowed — closest guess wins.
Balance 5 objects (each smaller than a 500ml water bottle) on top of each other. The stack must stand unsupported for 10 seconds. Fastest wins.
Find the height of your shortest teammate in sticks. Fastest wins.
All teammates must hold a full plank at the same time. The moment the first person stops, hit the finish button — the elapsed time is your result. LONGEST time wins.
Find an image of an extinct animal. It cannot be on a screen or electronic device, and not on anything owned by any player. Fastest wins.
One teammate makes pictures of animals on the ground from natural objects; another teammate must correctly name each animal attempted. Most animals wins.

Choose a teammate: they go away where they can't see or hear you (they may research while away). The rest create a scout tracking sign. The solo teammate returns and has ONE guess at what it means — a wrong guess is a fail. Fastest wins.
Pass Mark: Sign guessed on the first try

Challenge: Balance a natural object (stone, stick, pinecone…) on top of your head. The clock started when you pressed Start — hit the finish button the moment it falls off. LONGEST time wins.
How big is the Bushy Wood site in acres? No research allowed — closest guess wins.
